content.text: University of Damghan, Damghan, Iran (Sep 2013 - Sep 2016)
Electronic Structure and Quantum Transport in Impurity-Modified Phosphorene

This thesis investigated how atomic-scale defects and impurities modify the electronic structure and quantum transport properties of phosphorene, a two-dimensional semiconductor with strong anisotropy and a tunable band gap. The work combined first-principles electronic-structure calculations with quantum transport simulations to analyze how structural imperfections influence device-relevant behavior.

items: Electronic band structure of monolayer phosphorene.
Influence of point defects, vacancies, and substitutional dopants.
Spin-dependent electronic states and magnetization effects.
Edge physics in zigzag phosphorene nanoribbons.
Quantum transport through nanoscale junctions and hybrid graphene-phosphorene systems.
Density Functional Theory (DFT): equilibrium geometries, band structures, DOS/PDOS, impurity-induced localized states, magnetic moments, and charge redistribution.
Technical setup: GGA/PBE exchange-correlation functional, double-zeta polarized basis sets, supercell models for defects and dopants, structural relaxation, and total-energy minimization.
Defect and impurity modeling: halogen adatoms, substitutional dopants (H, N, O, F), monovacancies, dislocation-type defects, and impurity-vacancy interactions.
Electronic analysis: defect-induced mid-gap states, charge transfer, bonding interpretation, and spin-polarized effects.
Quantum transport via NEGF for zigzag phosphorene nanoribbons, H/O-passivated edges, graphene/phosphorene/graphene junctions, and phosphorene/graphene-flake/phosphorene devices.
Transport analysis: spin-resolved transmission spectra, electrode-channel coupling, and structure-transport correlations.

items.alt: Electronic band structures of perfect and defective phosphorene by dislocation of one P atom. The red and blue shaded area indicate the band structure of perfect phosphorene. The Fermi level is shown by green dotted lines and set to zero.
Projected density of state on the band structure. The colorful circles indicate the density of state for each case. The radius of this circle are coefficients of a value of DOS in that area.
Molecular junction’s structure before (right) and after (left) optimization. P atoms are denoted with yellow balls and C atoms with red ones.
P/Graphene/P’s structure before (right) and after (left) relaxation. P atoms are denoted with yellow balls and C atoms with red ones.
